definition: Breakeven inflation markets directionally underprice right-tail inflation risk, with a negative inflation risk premium (-10 to -20bp) inconsistent with asymmetric upside risks from tariffs, fiscal trajectory, and Phillips Curve convexity, though the specific magnitude is fragile due to TIPS liquidity premium uncertainty (-80bp to +40bp).
evidence: Fisher decomposition shows negative inflation risk premium of -10 to -20bp
Probability-weighted analogue analysis yields bimodal distribution making optionality cheap even if level approximately fair
TIPS liquidity premium ranges -80bp to +40bp, making any level claim fragile
Breakeven term structure flatness through 2Y-5Y belly inconsistent with both level and shelter resolution story
open_questions: What is the true TIPS liquidity premium?
Is inflation optionality the better expression than breakeven level trades?
